qa_pairs.question: How can Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injections be automated in Ansys Fluent using a journal file?
qa_pairs.answer: DPM injections can be automated by including specific commands in the journal file after solution initialization. This involves enabling coupled calculations, deleting existing injections, creating new injections from a specified file (e.g., `zinjection01.inj`), and then listing particle and injection properties. An example sequence of commands is `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/delete-injection injection-0:1`, and `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no`.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user view the available command-line options for `cfx5solve` in Ansys CFX?
qa_pairs.answer: To view a summary of `cfx5solve` command-line options, a user should first load the same module version that will be used in their Slurm script, and then run the command `cfx5solve -help` in the terminal.

qa_pairs.question: What command-line options are available for running Ansys CFX's `cfx5solve` with double precision or for simulations involving very large meshes?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, `cfx5solve` runs in single precision. To run in double precision, add the `-double` option to the command; however, this will also double memory requirements. For simulations with meshes containing up to 2 billion elements (exceeding the default limits), the `-large` option should be added to the command.
